title: NOC 73200 – Residential and commercial installers and servicers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 73200 professionals are the multi-talented technical experts responsible for the installation and repair of a wide variety of interior and exterior products. As a TEER 3 occupation, these specialists handle everything from major home appliances and security systems to overhead doors and pre-fabricated windows. In 2026, as Canada undergoes a massive "Smart Home" revolution and energy-efficient retrofitting boom, these workers are classified as Essential Service and Infrastructure Talent. For immigration, this role is a prime target for the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP) and the Saskatchewan Hard-to-Fill Skills Pilot.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level3Major Group73 – General tradesSub-Major Group732 – Building maintenance installers, servicers and repairersMinor Group7320 – Building maintenance installers, servicers and repairersNOC Code73200

duties: Blueprint Reading: Read and interpret blueprints, drawings, and manufacturer specifications to determine installation requirements and layout.
Product Installation: Install residential and commercial products such as windows, doors, electrical appliances, and security hardware using hand and power tools.
System Testing: Test installed products to ensure they are functioning safely and correctly according to manufacturer and local building codes.
Troubleshooting: Diagnose malfunctions and perform repairs on existing residential or commercial installations (e.g., repairing motor mechanisms in garage doors).
Measurement & Layout: Measure and mark guidelines to ensure the precise placement of products such as siding, shutters, or kitchen cabinets.
Structural Modification: Perform minor modifications to existing structures, such as cutting openings for windows or reinforced mounting for heavy appliances.
Client Education: Demonstrate the operation and maintenance of newly installed systems (e.g., smart thermostats or water softeners) to customers.
Routine Servicing: Perform scheduled maintenance on systems such as swimming pool filters, water conditioners, or heating equipment.
Documentation: Maintain accurate digital records of installations, service calls, and materials used in company management software.
Estimation: Provide on-site inspections and prepare detailed cost estimates for labor and materials to residential and commercial clients.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is the standard requirement.
Training: Completion of a one-to-three-year college program or extensive on-the-job training (2–3 years) is typically required.

Certifications:A valid Class 5 Driver’s License is almost always mandatory for field service roles.Gas Technician (G3/G2) license may be required for those installing gas appliances.Electrical Safety Authority (ESA) recognition or limited electrical certifications may be required for certain security or appliance roles.
A valid Class 5 Driver’s License is almost always mandatory for field service roles.
Gas Technician (G3/G2) license may be required for those installing gas appliances.
Electrical Safety Authority (ESA) recognition or limited electrical certifications may be required for certain security or appliance roles.
Bonding: Many employers require the ability to be bonded (criminal record check) since work is often performed inside private residences.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, specialized installers (e.g., security or high-end appliance technicians) often earn annual salaries between $62,000 and $88,000, with senior leads exceeding $95,000 inclusive of commissions.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that the sector will face a significant "Retirement Wave" over the next decade. As Canada mandates higher energy-efficiency standards for appliances and continues to expand its housing stock, the demand for technicians capable of high-precision installation and servicing will remain robust through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for installers and servicers in 2026 is Stable to Strong, driven by a record-high volume of residential housing completions and a surge in commercial renovations. In Ontario, British Columbia, and Alberta, the market is particularly active due to the transition to high-efficiency appliances and integrated home automation systems. According to 2026 Job Bank data, the industry is seeing a specific labor shortage for technicians specialized in heat pump installation and automated security hardware. Approximately 90% of workers in this NOC are employed full-time, offering high job security.
The "Smart Home" Era: Gain experience installing IoT-enabled appliances, smart security systems, and energy-monitoring hardware.
Rapid Workforce Entry: Unlike many heavy trades, this NOC allows for faster entry into the workforce with a combination of short-term training and on-the-job experience.
Diverse Specialization: Choose to specialize in high-demand niches like solar panel mounting, commercial overhead doors, or high-end kitchen appliances.
Strategic PR Pathways: This TEER 3 role is a recognized category for Provincial Nominee Programs (PNP) and the Federal Skilled Trades Program, often bypassing higher CRS requirements.
pathways: Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): A direct route for installers with 2 years of experience and a valid job offer or certificate of qualification.
Saskatchewan SINP (Hard-to-Fill Skills): A high-priority stream for installers with a valid job offer in the province.
OINP Employer Job Offer (In-Demand Skills): Frequently targets installers for regions outside the Greater Toronto Area.
Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): This NOC is a frequent target for category-based draws in 2026.
